bio.short: Fresh Graduate in Biochemistry from IPB University with hands-on experience in laboratory testing, analytical evaluation, and data documentation
bio.long: Fresh Graduate in Biochemistry from IPB University with extensive hands-on experience in molecular biology, nucleic acid analysis, and biomolecular characterization through laboratory assistantships and research internships. Proficient in DNA extraction, PCR, agarose and PAGE electrophoresis, NanoDrop and UV– Vis spectrophotometry, laboratory documentation, and Good Laboratory Practice (GLP).

education.description: Fresh Graduate in Biochemistry from IPB University with a strong interest in molecular biology, research and development, laboratory analysis, and quality control

projects.title: Independent Pilot Program
projects.subtitle: Horticultural Therapy Research
projects.description: Designed and implemented a horticultural therapy program at Rumah Azaki — a social welfare institution in Bogor — to develop self-care skills and social competencies for individuals with intellectual and mental disabilities. Demonstrated up to 80% improvement in planting activities for intellectually disabled participants.

projects.title: Natal CIVA 2023 IPB University
projects.subtitle: Logistics Committee
projects.description: Served as a Logistics Committee member for CIVA Christmas 2023, a collaborative Christmas celebration organized by KEMAKI and PMK IPB University. Responsible for coordinating the photobooth vendor, preparing the event venue, and ensuring all logistical arrangements were completed efficiently to support the smooth execution of the event.

projects.title: Ubiquinon 2024
projects.subtitle: Secretary of Funding and Sponsorship
projects.description: Served as the Secretary of the Fundraising and Sponsorship Division for Ubiquinon 2024, a national scientific innovation competition organized by the Biochemistry Student Association (CREBs) of IPB University. Responsible for preparing sponsorship proposals, documenting meeting minutes, managing administrative needs of the division, and organizing Zoom meetings to support effective coordination and communication.

experiences.description:
- Mentored and supervised 7 students during biochemistry laboratory practical sessions
- Performed demonstrations on agarose gel electrophoresis and laboratory instrument operation
- Assisted practical sessions involving paper chromatography, UV–Vis spectrophotometry, titration, biochemical assays, and genomic DNA analysis
- Ensured laboratory activities were conducted in accordance with laboratory safety and standard operating procedures (SOPs)
- Maintained accurate documentation and monitored the submission of laboratory reports

- Guided and supervised 12 students during laboratory practical sessions on carbohydrate, protein, lipid, and nucleic acid analysis, including starch and lipid hydrolysis, protein and nucleic acid denaturation, and biomolecule characterization
- Operated and assisted in the use of laboratory instruments, including a UV–Vis spectrophotometer, PCR machine, and agarose gel electrophoresis system
- Conducted student assessments in accordance with the course contract

experiences.role: Nucleic Acid and Protein Engineering Laboratory Assistant
experiences.description:
- Guided and supervised 11 students during gene isolation and cloning sessions of the amylase-coding gene from Bacillus licheniformis, covering genomic DNA extraction, PCR-based cloning, agarose gel electrophoresis, and plasmid DNA digestion
- Operated laboratory instruments including a PCR machine, NanoDrop spectrophotometer, and agarose gel electrophoresis system. Conducted student assessments in accordance with the course contract
- Conducted student assessments in accordance with the course contract

- Assisted analysis of 30+ salak leaf DNA samples using gradient PCR and agarose gel electrophoresis
- Assisted analysis of 41 oil palm leaf DNA samples using PCR and polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (PAGE)
- Supported oil palm self-DNA tissue culture research by contributing to the preparation of 100+ oil palm seedling samples, including sterilization and observation

- Performed moisture and phosphate analyses on 30+ plant samples using gravimetric and UV-VIS spectrophotometry, and prepared essential laboratory equipment, buffers, and chemical reagents
- Conducted protein and xylanase activity assays on 30+ service samples, ensuring accurate data generation
-Maintained compliant raw material storage and laboratory waste handling in accordance with safety and environmental protocols

- Conducted proximate and phytochemical analyses while strictly implementing Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) standards to ensure data accuracy and laboratory safety, including moisture/ash determination and antioxidant, flavonoid, and phenolic quantification using UV–VIS spectrophotometry
- Performed identification and characterization of 10+ samples using FTIR and TLC spectroscopy for chemical fingerprinting

experiences.company: Student Executive Board FMIPA - IPB University
experiences.description:
- Planned the main work program concepts, G-astip and Tutor BEM FMIPA, by organizing profit-sharing arrangements and implementation systems for both programs
- Achieved over 20 G-astip customers and successfully engaged 5 students in the Tutor BEM FMIPA
- Achieved a profit of Rp8.000.000,- in 10 months of management from an initial capital of Rp400.000,-.

experiences.role: Secretary of the Administrative Affairs Bureau
experiences.company: IPB Sustainable Science Research Student Association
experiences.description:
- Documented meeting minutes and prepared evaluation reports for the Bureau of Administrative Affairs
- Monitored and reviewed the budget plan (RKAT) and meeting records under the Directorate of Talent, Partnership, and External Communication

- Mentored a group of 10 KIP-K scholarship students (Batch 60) under the KIP Kuliah Mengabdi program at IPB University
- Guided mentees in navigating the KIP-K system and preparing for community service projects at their former or nearby schools in Bogor
- Supported the implementation of outreach activities to promote the KIP-K scholarship and inspired high school students to pursue higher education through equal opportunity pathways

publications.title: Pengembangan Bina Diri dan Keterampilan Sosial Penyandang Disabilitas Intelektual dan Mental Melalui Program Independent Pilot
publications.venue: AGROKREATIF: Jurnal Ilmiah Pengabdian kepada Masyarakat
publications.abstract: Rumah Azaki, a social welfare institution in Bogor Regency, faces challenges in facilitating individuals with disabilities to develop self-care skills and social competencies. The Independent Pilot program was designed to address these issues through a horticultural therapy approach, demonstrating up to 80% improvement in planting activities for intellectually disabled participants.

awards.title: Scientific Writing Competition 13th Airlangga Ideas Competition 2025
Business of The Year - Student Entrepreneurship Program (Batiknese)
National Student Creativity Program in Social Humanities Research (PKM-RSH) 2023
